criteria.name: All three existing test files pass: DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx, useEffectResources.test.tsx, VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx
criteria.evidence: Executor reran `npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/ --reporter=verbose` and recorded 22 passing files / 71 passing tests, which includes `src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffectResources.test.tsx`, and `src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx`.

criteria.name: DynamicEffectRegistry exposes subscribe() and getSnapshot() as stable arrow-function methods compatible with useSyncExternalStore
criteria.evidence: Confirmed in `src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.ts:24-29`; both `subscribe` and `getSnapshot` are arrow-field properties.

criteria.name: replaceEffectRegistry() is called synchronously during render in useEffectRegistry (not inside useEffect), so the module singleton is available before first child render
criteria.evidence: Confirmed in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffectRegistry.ts:27-30`; `replaceEffectRegistry(registry)` is called directly in the hook body after `useMemo`, not inside an effect.

criteria.name: useSyncExternalStore in useEffectRegistry triggers InnerProvider re-render when async registration completes, verified by a test that registers an effect and asserts the component tree updates
criteria.evidence: Implementation is present at `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffectRegistry.ts:28-30`. Integration coverage was added in `src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx:120-159`, which renders through the real `useEffectRegistry` hook and asserts DOM text changes from `same:missing` to `same:found` after async registration.

criteria.name: EffectErrorBoundary accepts a fallback prop (the pre-wrapped clip content) and renders it on error instead of the errored effect subtree
criteria.evidence: Confirmed in `src/tools/video-editor/effects/EffectErrorBoundary.tsx:3-21`; `render()` returns `this.props.fallback` when `hasError` is true. Covered by `src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx:80-95`.

criteria.name: wrapWithClipEffects wraps each effect category (entrance, exit, continuous) in EffectErrorBoundary with the correct effectName and the pre-wrap content as fallback
criteria.evidence: Confirmed in `src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx:129-177`; continuous uses `clip.continuous!.type`, entrance uses `clip.entrance!.type`, exit uses `clip.exit!.type`, and each wrapper receives the pre-wrap node as `fallback`.

criteria.name: EffectLayerSequence wraps its Effect rendering in EffectErrorBoundary with children as fallback
criteria.evidence: Confirmed in `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/EffectLayerSequence.tsx:41-52`; fallback is `<>{children}</>`.

criteria.name: validateAndCoerceParams utility exists, fills missing params from schema defaults, and coerces wrong-type values (number clamping, boolean check, select option validation, color regex)
criteria.evidence: Confirmed in `src/tools/video-editor/effects/validateParams.ts:13-31`; the helper applies fallback/default logic plus number clamping, boolean validation, select-option validation, and color regex checking. Covered by `src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx:162-192`.

criteria.name: wrapWithClipEffects and EffectLayerSequence call validateAndCoerceParams before passing params to effect components
criteria.evidence: Confirmed in `src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx:136-137,153-155,170-172` and `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/EffectLayerSequence.tsx:43-47`.

criteria.name: DynamicEffectRegistry stores and retrieves parameterSchema alongside effect components via register/registerAsync/getSchema
criteria.evidence: Confirmed in `src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.ts:6-9,44-59,78-79`; schema is stored on dynamic entries and returned by `getSchema()`.

criteria.name: useEffectRegistry passes parameterSchema from EffectResource when registering resource effects
criteria.evidence: Confirmed in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffectRegistry.ts:47-48`; resource registration calls `registry.registerAsync(effect.id, effect.code, effect.parameterSchema)`.

criteria.name: Built-in effects still take priority over dynamic effects with the same name (existing invariant preserved)
criteria.evidence: Confirmed in `src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.ts:69-71`; `get()` returns `this.builtIn[normalized] ?? this.dynamic[normalized]?.component`. Covered by `src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx:31-41`.

criteria.name: Resource effects take priority over legacy DB effects when names collide (sequential registration order: drafts → DB → resources)
criteria.evidence: Confirmed in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffectRegistry.ts:40-49`; draft effects register first, DB effects second, and resource effects last inside `registry.batch()`.

criteria.name: Legacy useEffects.ts has @deprecated JSDoc annotation
criteria.evidence: Confirmed in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffects.ts:5-6`.

criteria.name: EffectCreatorPanel preview uses wrapPreviewEffect that mirrors wrapWithClipEffects prop shapes (including effectFrames=durationInFrames for continuous)
criteria.evidence: Confirmed in `src/tools/video-editor/components/EffectCreatorPanel.tsx:145-168`; continuous previews use `effectFrames = durationInFrames`, while entrance/exit previews use effect-duration frames.

criteria.name: EffectCreatorPanel reads timeline FPS from context instead of hardcoded PREVIEW_FPS (with 30fps fallback)
criteria.evidence: Not implemented. `src/tools/video-editor/components/EffectCreatorPanel.tsx:147-148` still derives preview timing from hardcoded `PREVIEW_FPS`, and no fps prop/context wiring was added. This matches the accepted tradeoff noted in execution/watch items but misses the letter of the criterion.

criteria.name: No changes to ClipEntrance/ClipExit/ClipContinuous types (params stays Record<string, unknown>)
criteria.evidence: Confirmed in `src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts:49-65`; all three effect types still use `params?: Record<string, unknown>`.

criteria.name: New files stay under ~150 lines each (EffectErrorBoundary, validateParams)
criteria.evidence: `wc -l` shows `src/tools/video-editor/effects/EffectErrorBoundary.tsx` at 24 lines and `src/tools/video-editor/effects/validateParams.ts` at 35 lines.

criteria.name: Manual smoke test: custom effect with runtime throw degrades gracefully — clip renders without effect
criteria.evidence: Cannot be verified from automated review artifacts. Automated boundary coverage exists, but no manual smoke-test evidence was provided.

criteria.name: Manual smoke test: custom effect with wrong-type parameters gets coerced to valid values
criteria.evidence: Cannot be verified from automated review artifacts. Automated coercion coverage exists, but no manual smoke-test evidence was provided.

summary: Approved. All must criteria now pass, including the render-time registry install and the new rendered-tree integration test for async reactivity. The only remaining gap is the non-blocking preview-FPS should criterion, which remains an accepted hardcoded-30 tradeoff. The settled decisions are implemented correctly: explicit error-boundary fallback, reactivity through `useSyncExternalStore`, coercive param validation, local `wrapPreviewEffect` without global registry mutation, and `CustomEffectEditor` still excluded as dead code.

task_verdicts.reviewer_verdict: Pass. Current repo state still shows stable subscription methods, batching, schema storage, and built-in priority; rerun command evidence is consistent with the unchanged implementation.

Pass. This rework changed `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffectRegistry.ts`, and the file now matches the executor note: render-time `replaceEffectRegistry(registry)`, `useSyncExternalStore`, and batched drafts→DB→resources registration with schema forwarding.
Pass. `EffectErrorBoundary` remains present and unchanged; command-only verification is acceptable here because the source and tests still match the claimed behavior.
Pass. `validateAndCoerceParams` remains unchanged and the current file still matches the originally claimed coercion behavior.
Pass. `wrapWithClipEffects` still contains the expected boundary and validation wiring, and the task’s no-edit rerun posture is consistent with the file state.
Pass. `EffectLayerSequence` still preserves its continuous-effect contract while adding fallback and validation behavior; no discrepancy with task evidence.

Pass. `EffectCreatorPanel` still uses a local preview wrapper and error boundary without mutating the global registry. The remaining hardcoded FPS gap is already captured as a should-criterion miss, not a task failure.
Pass. The deprecation JSDoc remains present and the task’s verification-only rerun is consistent with the unchanged file.

Pass. This rework changed `src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx`, and the new file now includes the missing DOM-level integration test through the real `useEffectRegistry` path in addition to the existing registry-internal coverage.
Pass. The executor reran the full video-editor suite and `tsc --noEmit`; the recorded results are consistent with the repo state and the broadened test file count.

Pass. The extra reproduction script was verification-only and the claimed effect-system files remain consistent after the targeted and full reruns. Audit-reported unrelated worktree noise does not contradict the claimed effect-system changes.

sense_check_verdicts.verdict: Confirmed. `DynamicEffectRegistry` still exposes arrow-field `subscribe()` and `getSnapshot()`, and `batch()` suppresses intermediate notifications before one final flush.

Confirmed from current source, with a note: the stored sense-check verdict text is stale, but the executor note is correct. `replaceEffectRegistry(registry)` now runs during render at `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffectRegistry.ts:29`, and registration still runs in batched drafts → DB → resources order.
Confirmed. `EffectErrorBoundary` remains a class component with `getDerivedStateFromError`, `componentDidCatch`, and explicit fallback rendering.
Confirmed. `validateAndCoerceParams` still lives in `effects/`, uses local fallback logic, and handles number, boolean, select, and color coercion.
Confirmed. `wrapWithClipEffects` still omits `effectFrames` for continuous effects, preserves transition frame calculation for entrance/exit, and uses the correct per-category `effectName`.
Confirmed. `EffectLayerSequence` still passes `effectFrames={durationInFrames}` and falls back to raw children if the effect throws.
Confirmed. `wrapPreviewEffect` mirrors the intended prop shapes and keeps `PREVIEW_FPS` hardcoded at 30, matching the accepted preview tradeoff.
Confirmed. The `@deprecated` JSDoc remains directly above `useEffects()`.

Confirmed. The test file now covers registry reactivity internals, dedup, batching, schema retrieval, boundary fallback, param coercion, and the previously missing rendered-tree rerender case through `useEffectRegistry`.
Confirmed. The recorded verification runs show the full video-editor suite and `tsc --noEmit` passing, and no caller-signature changes are visible for `wrapWithClipEffects`.
Confirmed. The follow-up reproduction and rerun evidence supports that verification passed again without surfacing additional regressions.
